commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a broad `cloudchamber` cgroup substring to the shared container detector and updates its detector comment.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: with sentinel files absent, current main's cgroup matcher returns false for the reported `cloudchamber_v2/..._oci` sample. I did not establish a live failing current-main run inside Cloudflare Sandbox.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor live Cloudflare proof plus a maintainer branch choice; automation should not request repair markers while the external real-environment proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: Fix #96484: Detect Cloudflare Sandbox as container environment via cloudchamber cgroup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The container environment detection in `src/infra/container-environment.ts` uses cgroup regex matching to detect Docker, Podman, Kubernetes, and LXC containers. Cloudflare Sandbox (Cloudchamber) containers expose a `cloudchamber` or `cloudchamber_v2` cgroup entry that was not matched, causing diagnostics and runtime behavior to misidentify the environment as non-container.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added `cloudchamber` to the cgroup regex pattern so Cloudflare Sandbox containers are correctly detected as container environments. Updated the JSDoc to list all supported container runtimes including Fly Machines and Cloudflare Sandbox.

## Evidence

- The `containerEnvironmentCache` mechanism (lazy, cached) correctly returns `true` for Cloudflare Sandbox environments
- Existing container detection tests pass
- Detection order unchanged: Fly Machines env vars → sentinel files → cgroup regex
- TypeScript compilation verified clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: krissding
